title: Taylor Sheridan, 'Wind River,' and How to Write a Killer Movie | The Big Picture (Ep. 20)
format: Sean and Chris break down Taylor Sheridan's frontier trilogy before Sheridan himself comes on to talk directing his own script for the first time.

segments.heading: The Sheridan Trilogy
segments.note: Sicario, Hell or High Water, and Wind River

films.title: Wind River
films.note: Sheridan's directorial debut, closing out his self-described frontier trilogy. Chris calls it more John Ford than his first two scripts, wide open and mournful, until the last act detonates.

films.title: Sicario
films.note: Denis Villeneuve's film of Sheridan's script. Chris: an Apocalypse Now for the drug war, with Emily Blunt as the audience's moral compass getting dismantled by Benicio del Toro and Josh Brolin.

films.title: Hell or High Water
films.note: David Mackenzie directs Sheridan's bank-robbery script. Chris reads it as a ghost-town West: a depopulated frontier, brothers robbing banks to save their mother's farm from foreclosure.

segments.heading: The Jeremy Renner Case
segments.note: Chris makes his pitch for an underused actor

films.title: The Bourne Legacy
films.note: Chris's admitted minority position: he thinks Renner is genuinely excellent here.

films.title: Kill the Messenger
films.note: Sean's counterexample: a Renner vehicle he couldn't carry.

films.title: Avengers: Age of Ultron
films.note: The farmhouse scene with Linda Cardellini, about all the Marvel run gave him, per Chris.

films.title: Wind River
films.note: Sheridan on directing his own script for the first time: every decision is now his to make, with nobody else to delegate it to.
